title: Melt In Mouth Crispy Japanese Pork Kaiseki Course
description: A traditional Japanese dish from Hokkaido, featuring crispy pork in a savory broth, diabetic-friendly and made in one pot. This kaiseki course is a hearty and flavorful meal.
instructions: In a large pot, combine pork belly, mirin, soy sauce, sake, and sesame oil, and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the pork is tender.
Add green onions and ginger to the pot, and stir to combine.
Cover the pot with a lid, and steam over boiling water for 10 minutes, or until the pork is crispy on the outside and tender on the inside.
Remove the pot from the heat, and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Slice the pork into thin pieces, and serve with the savory broth.
Garnish with additional green onions, if desired.
Refrigerate or freeze any leftovers for later use.
